rationale: Official Kering press release on Gucci-L'Oreal beauty licence; factual corporate deal announcement, not public opinion.
Japan trade press frames the accelerated beauty handover as evidence of Kering's urgency to fix Gucci's underperforming beauty segment.
Korean financial outlet relays deal terms factually for investor audience; slump referenced only as background context.
Quantified third-party commercial demand (140k-copy sellout in 3 hours, 5.72M yuan presale) for ambassador cover, but ambassador/fandom coverage caps magnitude at 0.5.
Weibo fan reaction is explicitly mixed with pushback on overblown hype and fandom comparison friction; contested sentiment defaults neutral.
Viral Xiaohongshu consumer sentiment cites store closures, 'design increasingly boring' complaints, and a sharp quantified resale price crash.
Chinese marketing-industry community outlet critically questions whether Gucci's 12-hour livestream was substantive or empty spectacle.
Reports Gucci's withdrawal from Seoul duty-free locations as part of a broader global store-closure plan, signaling retrenchment momentum.
Purely descriptive ambassador-appointment write-up with no evaluative verdict; dated background context.
Factual financial-press coverage of Kering's turnaround plan and store-network reset; strategic reporting, not consumer sentiment.
